decisions.decision: Capture process start as a module-scoped monotonic baseline near the import-time runtime constants in `backend/server.py`, rather than using a wall-clock timestamp or a `run_server()`-local value, so request-time uptime stays stable across system clock changes and works for direct module-import and direct-router test paths.
alternatives_rejected: Store a wall-clock `time.time()` timestamp and compute uptime from absolute time.
Capture startup time inside `run_server()` and miss direct `route_request(...)` and direct `HTTPServer(..., BayesHandler)` usage.
Reset the process-start marker from `reset_state()` alongside in-memory market state.

decisions.decision: Implement uptime as a small shared helper that derives elapsed seconds from the module-level monotonic baseline at request time and returns a float rounded to three decimals, matching the backend's existing public timing style closely enough for later `/v1/health` assertions without collapsing the value to whole seconds.
alternatives_rejected: Expose the raw unrounded float directly from the monotonic delta.
Round uptime to an integer-like seconds count represented as a float.
Inline the elapsed-time calculation inside future route or payload builders.

decisions.decision: Place the new helper with the shared health payload builders, so later component and `/v1/health` helpers can compose it cleanly, while keeping this slice strictly scoped to module-start uptime groundwork and leaving `reset_state()`, legacy health payloads, router wiring, and tests untouched.
alternatives_rejected: Treat this step as route-local `/v1/health` work instead of shared helper groundwork.
Hide the helper in an unrelated utility area away from the health helpers that will consume it next.
Expand this slice to modify `/health`, `/healthz`, or test reset behavior now.

steps.task: Add a module-level monotonic process-start constant near the existing import-time runtime globals in `backend/server.py`, preserving a module-lifetime uptime baseline that is independent of `reset_state()`.
Implement a shared helper near the existing health payload builders that converts the stored monotonic baseline into a three-decimal float `uptime_seconds` value when called.

Review the change boundary to confirm this slice remains preparatory helper work only: no edits to `reset_state()`, no legacy health payload changes, no `/v1/health` router wiring, and no test updates in this child task.
